schema.appDescription: Calculate monthly professional tax deducted from salary. State-wise rates — Maharashtra ₹200/month, Karnataka ₹200/month, West Bengal ₹200/month.

howToSteps.name: Enter your details
howToSteps.text: Fill in the inputs for the Professional Tax Calculator.

faq.q: What is professional tax in India?
faq.a: Professional tax is a state-level tax on salaried employees, self-employed, and professionals. Maximum rate: ₹2,500/year (₹200–₹250/month). Employers deduct it from salary and deposit with the state government. It is deductible from taxable income under Section 16 of the Income Tax Act.

faq.q: Who pays professional tax in India?
faq.a: Employees working in states that levy professional tax: Maharashtra, Karnataka, West Bengal, Andhra Pradesh, Telangana, Tamil Nadu, Gujarat, Madhya Pradesh, and others. Some states (Delhi, Rajasthan, UP, Haryana) do not levy professional tax. Self-employed individuals are directly liable.

faq.q: Is professional tax deductible from income tax?
faq.a: Yes. Professional tax paid is fully deductible from gross salary under Section 16(iii) before computing income from salary. At ₹2,400/year, this saves ₹720 in tax (at 30% slab). A small but legitimate deduction available under both old and new tax regimes.

faq.q: Is the Professional Tax Calculator free?

faq.a: No. Calculations run in your browser.

examples.heading: ₹50,000/month salary in Maharashtra
examples.text: Maharashtra professional tax: ₹10,000–₹20,000 salary = ₹150/month; above ₹20,000 = ₹200/month. At ₹50,000 salary: ₹200/month (₹2,400/year). Deductible from income tax. At 30% bracket, saves ₹720 in income tax.

examples.heading: ₹2 lakh/month salary in Karnataka
examples.text: Karnataka professional tax: above ₹15,000/month = ₹200/month (₹2,400/year). The maximum is the same regardless of salary level. No professional tax in Delhi — salaried employees in Delhi save ₹2,400/year vs Maharashtra.
